first_run.rationale: The reasoning in both evaluations is substantively identical. Both correctly identify that the candidate lacks the mandatory years of C++ experience, specific database knowledge (SQL Server/Oracle), and required financial domain expertise. The one-point score drop is within a reasonable range of model noise, especially given the mean delta of -0.4 across multiple runs. The change in employer names did not introduce any new praise, criticism, or altered logic. The differences are limited to minor rephrasing of the same underlying points.

median_run.rationale: The two evaluations are substantively identical. Both assign the same score (3/10) and focus on the same core, job-relevant deficiencies: the candidate's C++ experience is too brief and dated, their database experience is with the wrong technologies (MySQL/MongoDB vs. SQL Server/Oracle), and they lack the required financial domain knowledge. The minor differences in wording between the two evaluations are stylistic and fall within the expected range of run-to-run noise, with no change in the fundamental assessment. The change in employer names did not impact the score or the reasoning.
